lesson_title: Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.back: A predefined set of bits used to manipulate (select, set, clear, or toggle) specific bits in a value
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.text: To test if a bit is set using a mask, use the {{c1::&}} operator and check if the result is {{c2::non-zero}} (or equal to the mask).
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.question: What does this code check?
cards.answer: Checks if bit 2 is set in flags
cards.explanation: The mask has only bit 2 set. Using & with the mask isolates bit 2. If that bit is set in flags, the result is non-zero (true).
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.text: To set (turn on) a bit using a mask, use the {{c1::|=}} operator: flags {{c1::|=}} mask;
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.text: To clear (turn off) a bit using a mask, use the {{c1::&=}} operator with the {{c2::inverted mask}}: flags {{c1::&=}} {{c2::~}}mask;
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.question: What is the value of flags after this operation?
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.question: What is the value of flags after this operation?
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.text: To toggle (flip) a bit using a mask, use the {{c1::^=}} operator: flags {{c1::^=}} mask;
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.question: What is the value of flags after two toggle operations?
cards.explanation:
First toggle flips bit 2 to 0: 0b0000'0001
Second toggle flips bit 2 back to 1: 0b0000'0101
XOR twice returns to original value.
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.text: To create a mask for bit n, use the left shift: {{c1::1 << n}} which creates a value with only bit n set.
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.question: What mask does this create?
cards.explanation:
1 << 3 shifts the bit 1 three positions left:
0000 0001 becomes 0000 1000
This creates a mask for bit 3.
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.concept: How to work with multiple bits at once using masks
cards.explanation:
You can combine multiple bit masks using the OR operator to create a mask that affects multiple bits at once.
  • Set multiple bits: flags |= (mask1 | mask2)
  • Clear multiple bits: flags &= ~(mask1 | mask2)
  • Test multiple bits: (flags & (mask1 | mask2)) != 0
examples: constexpr unsigned char mask = (1 << 2) | (1 << 4);  // bits 2 and 4
flags |= mask;  // set both bits 2 and 4
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.front: Why should bit masks be declared as constexpr?
cards.back:
Because masks are typically compile-time constants that don't change. Using constexpr:
  • Ensures compile-time evaluation
  • Allows use in constant expressions
  • Provides better optimization
  • Makes intent clear
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.question: What does this condition check?
cards.answer: Checks if BOTH bit 0 AND bit 4 are set
cards.explanation: Testing (flags & mask) == mask checks if ALL bits in the mask are set in flags. Both bits 0 and 4 must be 1 for the condition to be true.
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks

cards.text: The four key bit manipulation operations are: {{c1::test}} (check if set), {{c2::set}} (turn on), {{c3::clear/reset}} (turn off), and {{c4::toggle/flip}} (invert).
cards.source: O.3 - Bit manipulation with bitwise operators and bit masks
